req.title: Submit leave request — variant 2
req.statement: A full-time employee shall be able to submit a leave request for between 1 and 30 business days; requests spanning more than 30 days require hr-admin approval. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.
error_pathways: 422 when request exceeds 30 days without hr-admin

doc.reasoning: The requirement has three distinct partitions: (1) valid leave 1-30 days, (2) leave >30 days without hr-admin (422), and (3) invalid date range start>end (400) and overlapping leave (409). BVA targets the 30-day boundary exactly. STC covers the idempotency/retry guarantee. DT covers the hr-admin approval gate. NEG covers error pathways. Tests are written with pytest + Playwright page fixture and a REST API client fixture for setup/teardown, using placeholders for helpers.

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_valid_mid_range
preconditions: User is authenticated as a full-time employee
No existing leave overlaps the target date range
SMTP test sink is available to capture emails
actions: Fill end_date = start_date + 9 business days (10-day range, well within 1-30)
Capture the created leave request ID from the success banner
expected: Leave request record exists in the system with status=pending
Notification email delivered to employee email address
trace: R-WEB-058: valid full-time employee leave 1-30 days -> request created
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_valid_mid_range(page, auth_as_full_time_employee, api_client, clean_leave_state, smtp_sink):
    start, end = <get_next_business_day>(), <add_business_days>(start, 9)
    page.goto("/leave-requests/new")
    page.fill("[data-testid='start-date']", start.isoformat())
    page.fill("[data-testid='end-date']", end.isoformat())
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")
    banner = page.locator("[data-testid='success-banner']")
    banner.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=5000)
    request_id = banner.get_attribute("data-request-id")
    assert request_id, "Expected a leave request ID in success banner"
    record = api_client.get(f"/api/leave-requests/{request_id}").json()
    assert record["status"] == "pending"
    assert record["business_days"] == 10
    notifs = api_client.get("/api/notifications", params={"request_id": request_id}).json()
    assert any(n["type"] == "manager_notified" for n in notifs)
    emails = smtp_sink.messages_for(record["employee_email"])
    assert len(emails) >= 1

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_start_after_end_returns_400
preconditions: User is authenticated as a full-time employee
actions: Fill end_date = today + 2 business days (end before start)
expected: Form shows a validation error or page returns HTTP 400
Error message references invalid date range
No leave request record created in the system
trace: R-WEB-058 error pathway: 400 when start_date > end_date
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_start_after_end_returns_400(page, auth_as_full_time_employee, api_client, clean_leave_state):
    start = <add_business_days>(<today>(), 5)
    end = <add_business_days>(<today>(), 2)
    page.goto("/leave-requests/new")
    page.fill("[data-testid='start-date']", start.isoformat())
    page.fill("[data-testid='end-date']", end.isoformat())
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")
    error = page.locator("[data-testid='error-message']")
    error.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=5000)
    assert "date" in error.inner_text().lower()
    count = api_client.get("/api/leave-requests", params={"employee_id": <current_employee_id>()}).json()["total"]
    assert count == 0

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_exactly_30_days_boundary
preconditions: User is authenticated as a full-time employee
No existing leave overlaps the target date range
actions: Fill end_date = start_date + 29 business days (exactly 30 business days)
expected: Leave request created successfully (no hr-admin required at exactly 30 days)
trace: R-WEB-058: upper boundary of allowed range = 30 business days
R-WEB-058: >30 days requires hr-admin, so 30 must pass without it
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_exactly_30_days_boundary(page, auth_as_full_time_employee, api_client, clean_leave_state):
    start = <get_next_business_day>()
    end = <add_business_days>(start, 29)
    page.goto("/leave-requests/new")
    page.fill("[data-testid='start-date']", start.isoformat())
    page.fill("[data-testid='end-date']", end.isoformat())
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")
    banner = page.locator("[data-testid='success-banner']")
    banner.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=5000)
    request_id = banner.get_attribute("data-request-id")
    assert request_id
    record = api_client.get(f"/api/leave-requests/{request_id}").json()
    assert record["status"] == "pending"
    assert record["business_days"] == 30
    assert record.get("requires_hr_admin") is False

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_exceeds_30_days_without_hr_admin_returns_422
preconditions: User is authenticated as a full-time employee (NOT hr-admin)
No existing leave overlaps the target date range
actions: Fill end_date = start_date + 30 business days (31 business days total)
Submit the form without hr-admin elevation
expected: Form shows error or HTTP 422 is returned
Error message indicates hr-admin approval is required
trace: R-WEB-058 error pathway: 422 when request exceeds 30 days without hr-admin
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_exceeds_30_days_without_hr_admin_returns_422(page, auth_as_full_time_employee, api_client, clean_leave_state):
    start = <get_next_business_day>()
    end = <add_business_days>(start, 30)
    page.goto("/leave-requests/new")
    page.fill("[data-testid='start-date']", start.isoformat())
    page.fill("[data-testid='end-date']", end.isoformat())
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")
    error = page.locator("[data-testid='error-message']")
    error.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=5000)
    text = error.inner_text().lower()
    assert "hr" in text or "admin" in text or "approval" in text
    count = api_client.get("/api/leave-requests", params={"employee_id": <current_employee_id>()}).json()["total"]
    assert count == 0

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_overlapping_returns_409
preconditions: User is authenticated as a full-time employee
An existing approved leave request covers next Monday through next Monday + 4 business days
actions: Pre-create overlapping leave via API fixture
Fill end_date = next Monday + 2 business days (overlaps existing)
expected: Form shows a conflict error or HTTP 409 is returned
No new leave request record created
trace: R-WEB-058 error pathway: 409 when overlapping existing leave
R-WEB-058 STC: state transition from submitted -> rejected(conflict) when overlap exists
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_overlapping_returns_409(page, auth_as_full_time_employee, api_client, existing_leave_fixture):
    start = <get_next_business_day>()
    end = <add_business_days>(start, 2)
    existing_leave_fixture.create(start=start, end=<add_business_days>(start, 4))
    page.goto("/leave-requests/new")
    page.fill("[data-testid='start-date']", start.isoformat())
    page.fill("[data-testid='end-date']", end.isoformat())
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")
    error = page.locator("[data-testid='error-message']")
    error.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=5000)
    text = error.inner_text().lower()
    assert "overlap" in text or "conflict" in text or "existing" in text
    total = api_client.get("/api/leave-requests", params={"employee_id": <current_employee_id>()}).json()["total"]
    assert total == 1

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_idempotent_under_retry
preconditions: User is authenticated as a full-time employee
No existing leave overlaps the target date range
Idempotency key support is enabled on the endpoint
actions: Capture the idempotency key embedded in the form (or generate one)
Submit the form once and record the returned request ID
Submit the identical form a second time with the same idempotency key
Compare the two returned request IDs
expected: Both submissions return the same leave request ID
Only one leave request record exists in the system
Manager receives exactly one notification (no duplicate)
trace: R-WEB-058: action must be idempotent under retry
R-WEB-058 STC: submitted -> submitted (retry) -> same terminal state, no duplicate
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_idempotent_under_retry(page, auth_as_full_time_employee, api_client, clean_leave_state):
    start = <get_next_business_day>()
    end = <add_business_days>(start, 4)
    idem_key = <generate_uuid>()
    def _submit():
        page.goto("/leave-requests/new")
        page.fill("[data-testid='start-date']", start.isoformat())
        page.fill("[data-testid='end-date']", end.isoformat())
        page.fill("[data-testid='idempotency-key']", idem_key)
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")
        banner = page.locator("[data-testid='success-banner']")
        banner.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=5000)
        return banner.get_attribute("data-request-id")
    id_first = _submit()
    id_second = _submit()
    assert id_first == id_second, "Retry must return the same request ID"
    result = api_client.get("/api/leave-requests", params={"employee_id": <current_employee_id>()}).json()
    assert result["total"] == 1
    notifs = api_client.get("/api/notifications", params={"request_id": id_first}).json()
    manager_notifs = [n for n in notifs if n["type"] == "manager_notified"]
    assert len(manager_notifs) == 1
